reviews.text: Entering into perimenopause, I was experiencing a drop in energy, focus and other effects. After all blood work was assessed, I started treatment. I feel so much better in many aspects of my life. I’m so grateful for Jessica and staff! They are professional and make the visits quick and easy. I would definitely recommend to anyone, especially women, who are starting to feel the impact of hormonal changes.

reviews.name: places/ChIJLywt0iYfsocRwanKSyN-Lyg/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT25KWlgweFNXVmd4TW5KNFJraG1kWEZ4WmtaNVNIYxAB
reviews.text: Went in for a simple shot. Extremely disappointing experience. I waited in the office for nearly two hours without ever being seen by the provider. When I asked the front desk staff for an update, they offered no information or reassurance. The medical assistant who came in to take my vitals didn’t bother to introduce herself, which added to the lack of professionalism. Eventually, I had to leave to return to work and informed the front desk, but was met with complete silence and no acknowledgment. The overall lack of communication and courtesy was unacceptable.

reviews.text: I simply called in to ask questions about weight loss options/coverage regarding my insurance and the lady that answered was not pleasant and had the nastiest tone. 1/10 I do not recommend. The foundation is set by the first impression whether I am in person or on the phone.

reviews.name: places/ChIJNzicwyYWsocRLDCyppGUw0s/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2xaVmF5MHhSM1ZaZUZoU1VrVjJhWGRITldSRk1uYxAB
reviews.text:
My wife was under the care of Dr. Angelia Casteel for Graves’ disease, and our experience was deeply frustrating, concerning, and in our opinion, medically mismanaged. Despite being trained in endocrinology, Dr. Casteel demonstrated a troubling lack of understanding in how to properly manage and monitor a patient with this condition.

From our very first visit, we clearly stated that our goal was to move toward a thyroidectomy, not long-term medication. Yet Dr. Casteel repeatedly ignored that treatment plan and instead pushed a long-term regimen of high-dose methimazole (50mg daily) without a clear path forward. She later acted as though surgery was never discussed, even though we had been upfront from the beginning. This left us feeling dismissed and misled. Whether this was due to miscommunication, negligence, or a financial motive to prolong care through copays and follow-ups, we do not know for sure.

What we do know is that Dr. Casteel failed to order complete thyroid panels, especially Free T3, which is crucial in the treatment of Graves' disease. She also failed to monitor critical safety labs like liver function tests and CBCs in a timely manner, despite prescribing a high-risk medication. When we finally pushed for these labs ourselves, they showed my wife was biochemically hypothyroid and clearly overmedicated, explaining her increasing fatigue and worsening symptoms.

When I, as her husband, respectfully advocated for her care, I was met with resistance and defensiveness. Dr. Casteel insisted that only TSH and Free T4 mattered, which contradicts well-established clinical guidelines that emphasize the importance of Free T3 monitoring in hyperthyroid patients, particularly those with Graves’.

It was only through our own efforts, along with support from our primary care physician, that we were able to get a second opinion and begin correcting the course of treatment.

This review is not meant to be spiteful, but to serve as a serious warning to other patients and families. Graves’ disease requires attentive, evidence-based care. In our experience, Dr. Casteel failed to meet that standard. Beware of overtreatment, dismissive behavior, and a lack of appropriate follow-through. Patients deserve better.

Update(7/30/25): After posting this review, I was contacted directly by Dr. Choe, the head of the practice. He acknowledged our concerns and stated that the matter is being addressed internally. I appreciate the fact that he took the time to follow up and take the situation seriously.

If you are seeking care at this practice, I recommend requesting Dr. Choe himself or one of the other providers. Based on our experience, I strongly advise others to be cautious with or avoid Dr. Casteel entirely.

reviews.name: places/ChIJe2y3rhHF7IART4TMrJ7cEx0/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2pBMWNsa3RkbTVQUzFCSmExbHNNRGd6Y1ZGSmQyYxAB
reviews.text:
I live in South Carolina, but my family is in Oklahoma City. When I discovered Dr. Ramsey, I knew immediately that my grandma needed to see him. What I didn’t expect was just how much he would change not only her life, but our entire family’s as well.

My grandma had been through it all with the traditional medical system—gallbladder removal, hysterectomy, fibromyalgia, a heart attack, countless prescriptions (some even dangerous). No one could truly help her. Even as a holistic functional practitioner/wellness coach myself, I couldn’t give her the relief she so desperately needed.

But Dr. Ramsey did. He’s completely transformed her health. For years, I never once got a phone call from her without hearing pain or exhaustion in her voice. Now? She calls to tell us about driving herself around, cooking, shopping, and visiting family—things I never thought I’d hear her say again.  Every time I had questions or thoughts as we continued helping her heal, he never left me hanging.  He answers every email, every call and moves us through our next steps.  I see this every day, people either neglect their health or the doctors gave them no true help, and so they end up with years of issues.  It takes a good amount of time to help your body uncover these issues.  We are still working to help her body become fully functional, but I am so confident in Dr. Ramsey because of what he has done for her in the short time we have worked with him.

If you are ready to take your health seriously, naturally, and wholeheartedly, you need to see him. Doctors in the conventional system are trained to medicate, not to heal.  I can confidently say this: Dr. Ramsey doesn’t charge nearly enough for what he gives. Because truly restoring your health - That is priceless.

Thank you thank you.  You are the reason I have learned so much and why people will be able to heal no matter what they are going through.

reviews.text:
I had been on prescription acid reflux medicine for over a decade. At the urging of my wife, I consulted with Dr. Ramsey on a natural approach to wean myself off the medication while taking whole food/herbal supplements. After four months, I am happy to say that I am largely medication free thanks to the program Dr. Ramsey had me do.

If you are looking for a natural solution for health problems sans medication, then I would recommend seeing Dr. Ramsey at the Functional Medicine Center.  It will most likely be more expensive than seeing a traditional insurance covered provider, but the goal is recovery and not only symptom treatment by dependence on a prescription.

reviews.name: places/ChIJe2y3rhHF7IART4TMrJ7cEx0/reviews/ChdDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSURTcDZUSDBnRRAB
reviews.text: I can't begin to say enough good things about Dr. Ramsey & his practice. I originally went to him for a horribly tweaked neck which he treated to almost 100% in one visit. Because my experience with this injury was so good I continued to visit him regarding some other pain I was having in my back. Not only am I feeling SO much better after a few months of treatments but I have also started utilizing the functional medicine aspect of his practice. I've uncovered some other issues I had going on and have found out some remarkable things that have been truly life-changing for me. Dr. Ramsey is extremely knowledgeable about what he does. He stays up on current research, continues to learn new things and is extremely informative. He gives his full attention to your visit and walks you though anything you might have questions or concerns about. His wife, Donna, who runs the front desk is also absolutely fabulous. She's one of the most warm and friendly people I have interacted with. She is also on top of all billing, insurance and scheduling questions. I could go on and on about how much I've loved my experience with this practice. You are in great hands with Dr. Ramsey & Donna.

reviews.text: Dr. Cassie and her staff at Modern Endocrine have helped me change my overall health. With Dr. Cassie’s guidance, my thyroid and hormones are under control and I feel SO much better. Not only have I lost weight, but I also have improved energy and sleep. Sarah and Dustin have been instrumental in getting my gut health in check. The inflammation in my body has lessened considerably but I am still a work in progress. Allen and the ladies at the front desk are extremely courteous and attentive to all of my scheduling and supplement needs. Thank you, Modern Endocrine!

reviews.name: places/ChIJYRKW8BcZsocRqe_8Ci34Y00/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2s5NVNrcExiMlZ1Wmt4TVVIcGxTR3haVGxOeWQwRRAB
reviews.text:
Very disorganized practice. I pressed 2 to get refills, and the young woman lied twice about the status of my refill. She didn't do any investigation. I had ordered through a mail order pharmacy and it was taking more than two weeks to get the refill. The doctor should tell patients about the delay.

When I called the third time, I was able to talk to the PA and he called in the prescription locally. However it was wrong because the nurse didn't write down the new strength that the doctor ordered. It will be nearly impossible to get this corrected, so I'm going to take the old strength.

I cancelled my next appointment (at least, I left a message because it's impossible to contact the front desk) and will have Optimal Health handle my medication. Should have done that in the first have place.

reviews.name: places/ChIJYRKW8BcZsocRqe_8Ci34Y00/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2pCUVVFRlBVV3RrUkhKME5uWlhPWHBpUVd3NGQyYxAB
reviews.text:
I’m about 4 months into the Gut Health Accelerator program at Modern Endocrine, and it has honestly been one of the best decisions I’ve made for my health. I had been struggling with digestion issues for about a year and tried so many different things without lasting results. This program is what finally helped me start feeling better.

The GI-MAP test was incredibly informative, and reviewing the results with Sara made a huge difference. She took the time to explain everything clearly and helped me understand what was actually going on and how to address it. The personalized, structured plan along with the monthly check-ins has been key in keeping me consistent and on track.

If you’ve been dealing with ongoing gut or digestion issues and feel stuck, I highly recommend Modern Endocrine and the Gut Health Accelerator program.

reviews.text:
I was very disappointed in the services I received at this practice. I began earlier this year feeling hopeful after a positive consultation with Dr. Holler, who assured me I would receive thorough care and a clear path toward feeling better. Unfortunately, that was not my experience.

I did not receive a structured or comprehensive healing plan. Instead, I was given numerous scattered suggestions that felt overwhelming and lacked cohesion. There was also a significant error in my chart—an established diagnosis I’ve had for 20 years was listed as “non-diagnosed.” When I brought this to Dr. Edwards’ attention, his response was “that would only be on my chart if I had told them that.” This felt unprofessional and dismissive.

I also completed an additional test at Dr. Edwards’ recommendation after being told it would provide valuable insight. When the results came back, I was told there was “little” to take from them, yet another practitioner later reviewed the same results and offered a much clearer, more actionable interpretation.

I raised my concerns about the care I was receiving on two occasions. After the first time, I was scheduled for an appointment, but the outcome was again a list of many suggestions with no real plan. After voicing my concerns a second time, I was removed from the membership.

Ultimately, my time and money were not well spent here. I have since found a practitioner who provides a clear, manageable healing plan and truly supportive care. Based on my experience, I would encourage those with chronic or complex health issues to seek care elsewhere if they are looking for thorough guidance, clarity, and follow-through. The level of care initially promised to me was not at all delivered and I felt more like a burden than a cared for patient.

reviews.name: places/ChIJQ1MKN7qStocRmZ9ozjdcQAQ/reviews/ChdDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSUMycWNHRGlRRRAB
reviews.text:
Before I began to write this review, I read several from other individuals. It's interesting to see so many varying points of view, but overwhelmingly mostly positive. The Center for Men is a locally owned small business. I too own a small business, with similar reviews and feedback. I guess no one can please everyone; no matter how well you do your work, there will always be someone who - based on their own experiences and history - will take issue with it. For whatever reason, what is acceptable and fine to many is not to them.

I am not one of those people in this case.

I have been a patient at the Center for Men for a little over two years now. I originally went there because I kept hearing advertisements for other places in town (to this day I don't believe I've ever heard a single advertisement for the Center for Men, even once.) These advertisements relentlessly played on the radio and I kept hearing many of the symptoms I had been experiencing. I was 49 and wondered if maybe I had low testosterone. After looking around at a few places I decided I liked the low pressure professional experience at Center for Men. I went and had my first labs and sure enough, I had low T.

I pay for it out of pocket and have been very satisfied with the quality of care I get there. I saw Dr. Price on the first visit, and have seen him on (I believe) every visit since the first one. I go for labs every three months and self inject at home.

It has changed my life. Quality time with my wife has never been better. The biggest thing for me was a lift of the "brain fog" that seemed to plague me. My work got better which has helped my home life.

Dr. Price and his crew have really helped me tremendously. I appreciate them very much and have recommended (and will continue to recommend) them to my friends.

I will add that as a small business owner, I understand the need to change pricing and options from time to time as dictated by price changes for supplies and labor. We all experience this - prices on eggs and gas go up and down but we don't blame it on QT or the grocery store. Center for Men has different pricing options, I just pay fee for service and buy my medications. They have never tried to strong arm me into another platform.

reviews.text:
My T used to be at 218, and I felt like everything I did was an act of sheer willpower.   Now it's over 500 and I feel great, have tons of energy, and I no longer feel like I'm moving in quicksand.

I'm a 50 year old male, and I've been using Craft Medical for Testosterone Replacement Therapy (TRT) since December of 2020.  They are absolutely the best!  All visits with the doctor are REMOTE - so, no missing work, no wasting time in a waiting room, no hassle.

I get labs every 90 days and a visit with my doctor to see if my dosage needs adjusted (I've had 2 tweaks so far), so that I get the results I want.  When it's time to schedule a lab, Craft Medical calls me, then they schedule the lab appointment at a lab near me.  My longest lab visit so far,  from the time I walked in the door, until the time I walked out, was 9 minutes.

Craft Medical has several options for TRT, but I'm not a big fan of needles, so the troches (TROE - keez) are what I use. Troches are a cinnamon or peppermint flavored lozenge that dissolve under your tongue, daily. It takes about 10 minutes to dissolve, so taking it is no big deal, and it keeps my T levels steady.
